title: B-Splines (Basis Splines)
body: B-Splines (Basis Splines) are a type of curve used in computer graphics and design. They are defined by a set of control points which act like 'magnets' to pull the curve into shape. Unlike some other types of curves, B-splines do not necessarily pass through their control points. One of the main advantages of B-splines is 'local control', meaning that moving one control point only affects a small part of the curve, making them very flexible for design.
